document.title: IEC 60601-1:2005+AMD1:2012+AMD2:2020 CSV
document.documentType: International medical electrical equipment safety standard

sourceSummary.oneLine: This chunk provides IEC 60601-1 rationale and annex material for PEMS, ME systems, mechanical and transformer tests, EMC, marking, leakage-current measurement, and flammable anaesthetic mixture protection.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports safety and regulatory documentation by identifying IEC 60601-1 process, test, marking, validation, and documentation expectations for medical electrical equipment and systems.
keyPoints: Clause 14 requires PEMS safety to be addressed through documented risk management, life-cycle, verification, validation, and controlled documentation processes.
Clause 16 requires ME systems, including combinations with non-ME equipment, to be assessed so the assembled or modified system does not create unacceptable risk.
Clause 17 relies on IEC 60601-1-2 for electromagnetic immunity, emissions, marking, and documentation requirements.
Annex B provides an informative sequence of testing and allows radiation, biocompatibility, usability, alarm systems, PEMS, and EMC tests to be performed independently.
Annex C and Annex D summarize marking, labelling, symbols, safety signs, and accompanying document requirements.
Annex G sets normative requirements for protection against ignition hazards from flammable anaesthetic mixtures.

entities.name: International Electrotechnical Commission
entities.evidence: Page headers and copyright identify IEC 60601-1:2005+AMD1:2012+AMD2:2020 CSV and IEC 2020.

entities.name: Manufacturer
entities.evidence: Clause 14 requires the MANUFACTURER's internal assessment and documentation in the RISK MANAGEMENT FILE.

entities.name: Responsible Organization
entities.evidence: Subclause 15.3.7 states the RESPONSIBLE ORGANISATION has to perform periodic inspection and maintenance prescribed by the MANUFACTURER.

entities.name: Certification body
entities.evidence: Subclause 14.2 states Clause 14 documentation is necessary for a certification body to determine that PROCESS steps have been performed.

entities.name: Test house
entities.evidence: Subclause 16.1 states standards compliance documentation can be a declaration of conformity by the manufacturer or a certificate from a test house.

concepts.name: Programmable Electrical Medical Systems
concepts.evidence: Clause 14 says testing the finished product alone is not adequate for PROGRAMMABLE ME EQUIPMENT MEDICAL ELECTRICAL SYSTEMS.

concepts.name: Risk Management File
concepts.evidence: Clause 14 documentation is required to be in the RISK MANAGEMENT FILE.

concepts.name: PEMS validation
concepts.evidence: PEMS VALIDATION is intended to assure that the right product is built and can include safety, security, performance, and fault testing.

concepts.name: Network/data coupling
concepts.evidence: Additional guidance on NETWORK / DATA COUPLING incorporation of a PEMS into an IT-NETWORK is found in Annex H.

concepts.name: ME systems
concepts.evidence: Clause 16 provides requirements to ensure safety of the patient who could come into contact with ME SYSTEMS.

concepts.name: Electromagnetic compatibility
concepts.evidence: IEC 60601-1-2 specifies electromagnetic immunity test levels and emissions limits for ME EQUIPMENT and ME SYSTEMS.

concepts.name: Sequence of testing
concepts.evidence: Annex B states tests should be performed in the indicated sequence unless otherwise stated by particular standards.

concepts.name: Marking and labelling requirements
concepts.evidence: Annex C is a guide to marking and labelling requirements for ME EQUIPMENT and ME SYSTEMS.

concepts.name: Symbols and safety signs
concepts.evidence: Annex D explains symbols are used on ME EQUIPMENT to avoid language differences and improve comprehension.

concepts.name: Flammable anaesthetic mixture ignition protection
concepts.evidence: Annex G is normative and covers protection against hazards of ignition of flammable anaesthetic mixtures.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 Clause 14 requires process-based controls for PEMS because finished-product testing alone is inadequate for programmable medical electrical systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 14 states that systematic failures can escape practical testing limits and that testing of the finished product is not, by itself, adequate.

evidence.claim: PEMS design must be performed within a risk management process according to ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 14.1 states the standard requires application of a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS in accordance with ISO 14971 and that PEMS design has to be performed within it.

evidence.claim: IEC 62304 software safety and software risk management requirements are incorporated by reference for PEMS software.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 14.1 includes software safety requirements from IEC 62304 subclause 4.3 and software risk management requirements from IEC 62304 Clause 7.

evidence.claim: Clause 14 compliance is judged by documentation review, and all Clause 14 documentation is required in the Risk Management File.
evidence.evidenceText: Compliance is judged by examining documentation produced by required processes; all documentation is required in the RISK MANAGEMENT FILE.

evidence.claim: PEMS documentation must be generated, revised, and maintained under a formal document control system.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 14.2 requires documents to be generated, revised and maintained under a formal document control system.

evidence.claim: A PEMS risk management plan must include a PEMS validation plan in addition to ISO 14971 plan elements.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 14.3 states a PEMS VALIDATION plan is required because validation is a necessary activity when developing a PEMS.

evidence.claim: PEMS life-cycle activities must define milestones, inputs, outputs, and verification criteria.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 14.4 requires milestones and activities with inputs and outputs so verification of results can be performed.

evidence.claim: PEMS architecture specifications are additional requirements beyond ISO 14971 because architecture may be part of risk control.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 14.8 states an architecture specification is additional for PEMS because the chosen architecture will often be part of a RISK CONTROL measure.

evidence.claim: PEMS validation can include security, performance, configuration compatibility, fault testing, documentation, and safety tests.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 14.11 lists high volume data, heavy loads or stresses, human factors, security, performance, configuration compatibility, fault testing, documentation and safety.

evidence.claim: PEMS connected by network/data coupling to other equipment require consideration of IT-network incorporation guidance.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 14.13 discusses hospitals operating ME EQUIPMENT in IT-NETWORKS and refers to Annex H for NETWORK / DATA COUPLING guidance.

evidence.claim: Enclosures must resist push, impact, drop, and rough-handling stresses where required to prevent unacceptable risk.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclauses 15.3.2 to 15.3.5 discuss push, impact, drop, and rough handling tests for enclosure rigidity, misuse, normal use, and rough handling.

evidence.claim: For internal electrical power sources, a short circuit test is required unless two means of operator protection are present between terminals and the protective device.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 15.4.3.5 states a short circuit test is required to demonstrate SINGLE FAULT SAFE unless two MEANS OF OPERATOR PROTECTION are present.

evidence.claim: Transformers used to provide separation are tested when used to separate operators, patients, or others from a hazard.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 15.5 says transformer tests should be used any time the transformer establishes separation between OPERATORS, PATIENTS, etc. and a HAZARD.

evidence.claim: FIW in transformers complying with IEC 62368-1:2018 is considered acceptable only for MOOP, while MOPP use needs further investigation.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 15.5.3 states FIW is acceptable for MEANS OF OPERATOR PROTECTION only and MOPP use needs further investigation.

evidence.claim: Clause 16 requires ME systems after installation or modification not to result in unacceptable risk.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 16.1 states the basic requirement is that, after installation or subsequent modification, an ME SYSTEM does not result in an unacceptable RISK.

evidence.claim: Appropriate documentation of ME system standards compliance may be a manufacturer declaration of conformity or a test-house certificate.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 16.1 states documentation concerning standards compliance can be a declaration of conformity by the MANUFACTURER or a certificate from a test house.

evidence.claim: Within the patient environment, ME systems must apply the same maximum patient leakage current values as ME equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause 16.6.3 states the same maximum values for PATIENT LEAKAGE CURRENT and total PATIENT LEAKAGE CURRENT apply.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1-2 specifies EMC immunity, emissions, identification, marking, and documentation requirements for ME equipment and ME systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 17 states IEC 60601-1-2 specifies immunity test levels, emissions limits, and requirements for identification, marking and documents.

evidence.claim: Annex B permits PEMS, EMC, radiation, biocompatibility, usability, and alarm-system tests to be performed independently from the listed test sequence.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex B states tests for radiation hazards, biocompatibility, usability, alarm systems, PEMS, and EMC can be performed independently.

evidence.claim: Annex C consolidates marking and accompanying-document requirements for ME equipment and ME systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex C tables list outside markings, inside markings, controls, accompanying documents, instructions for use, and technical description requirements.

evidence.claim: Annex D distinguishes informational symbols from safety signs and requires safety signs where warnings are intended.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex D states Table D.2 safety signs are required where a warning is intended, while Table D.1 symbols are used solely to inform.

evidence.claim: Annex G is normative and sets requirements for ME equipment used where flammable anaesthetic mixtures may create explosion risk.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex G states an explosion RISK can exist where flammable anaesthetics or agents are mixed with air, oxygen, or nitrous oxide.

evidence.claim: ME equipment specified for use with flammable anaesthetic mixture with oxygen or nitrous oxide must be Category APG equipment and comply with G.4 and G.6.
evidence.evidenceText: Subclause G.2.5 states such ME EQUIPMENT or parts shall be CATEGORY APG ME EQUIPMENT and shall comply with G.4 and G.6.

reviewFlags.reason: This is chunk 11 of 13 and contains rationale plus annex excerpts; extraction is limited to claims visible in this chunk.
Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but this chunk itself identifies IEC standards content rather than an FDA-issued document.
